rationale: Both candidates independently surface a real async_chunk getattr default-inconsistency (True vs False) that echoes the ground-truth 'Low' robustness concern, and both validate the qwen3_tts scoping fix (echoing the 'Med' thread). Neither candidate raises amy-why's design question about moving the qwen3-specific variable into the model. X pads its review with several low-value, weakly-grounded asks ('please re-run this specific pytest invocation', claims about a 'PR's manual test command' not present in the given diff context) that read as fabricated/unverifiable filler, hurting precision. Y is tighter and better grounded, verifies is_streaming_request's actual scope, and crucially flags that the split now keys solely on total_num_scheduled_tokens and would miss tensors aligned to hidden_states.shape[0] instead — directly hitting the latent dual-batch-axis gap later fixed by PR #4910, even though Y (perhaps having peeked at post-merge main) prematurely waves it off as already handled.
